items.title: Cardano hands core development to outside teams in decentralization push
items.description: Input Output will transfer control of the Haskell node, Plutus, Hydra and other components as founder Charles Hoskinson says the network must change and start growing again.

items.title: Inside Robinhood’s high-stakes bet to onboard 10 million casual users onto decentralized finance
items.description: The company says its advantage lies in bringing retail customers onchain, but early activity remains dominated by memecoins, while its original tokenization vision remains small.

items.title: Japan's SBI Group is building Asia's first cross-border digital asset empire
items.description: The securities giant announced today the consolidation of Singapore-based Coinhako as part of a rapid regional expansion that includes a tokenization partnership with Ondo Finance.

items.title: Bitcoin faces fresh headwinds as China’s Kimi beats Claude, GPT in coding benchmark
items.description: Moonshot's Kimi K3 took the top spot in frontend coding away from Claude and OpenAI, and it's free. Semiconductor stocks fell, and crypto fell with them.

items.title: AI frenzy losing steam leaves bitcoin less volatile than South Korean stocks
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 17, 2026

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin returns to $63,000 as Nasdaq trims large early loss
items.description: A deepening global selloff in chipmakers dragged risk assets lower, pulling bitcoin back from the $65,000 level it reached on this week's soft inflation print.

items.title: Risk-off wave drags bitcoin below $63,000 as AI selloff spreads from stocks to crypto
items.description: Crypto fell as AI stock fatigue and U.S.-Iran tensions weighed on sentiment, though a technical measure hints at oversold conditions heading into the weekend.

items.title: Airbnb CEO says X account was hacked, attacker posted AI-slop on tokenization
items.description: Brian Chesky regained control of his account and told any new crypto followers he would be a "disappointing follow".

items.title: Bitcoin price sags under $62.5K as Iran strikes add to US stocks pressure
items.description: Bitcoin saw a key rejection at local highs before reversing lower, moving with stocks for a second day as US-Iran war downside took its toll.

items.title: HSBC wins Bank of England approval to enter Digital Securities Sandbox
items.description: The Bank of England approved HSBC Orion to go live in its Digital Securities Sandbox, with the first Digital Gilt Instrument transaction expected in the first quarter of 2027.

items.title: SBI acquires Singaporean crypto platform Coinhako after MAS approval
items.description: SBI Holdings received regulatory approval to acquire a majority stake in Singapore-based crypto exchange Coinhako as it expands into stablecoins, onchain finance and tokenized assets.

items.title: The British Virgin Islands are a top crypto hub no one ever talks about: Here’s why
items.description: Kraken, Bitstamp, 1inch and Bitfinex have all set up shop in the British Virgin Islands — but you might find it tricky to book an on-site meeting with one of their executives.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Dutch court declares crypto platform Knaken bankrupt over missing funds
items.description: The Rotterdam court said bankruptcy was necessary to ensure an orderly settlement because the company lacked enough assets to fully repay users.

items.title: Stablecoin growth will erode bank deposits, says ECB’s Cipollone
items.description: ECB’s Piero Cipollone said stablecoin adoption could erode bank deposits, but the digital euro will keep banks at the center of payments.

items.title: UK sentences 2 hackers tied to $115M crypto ransom scheme
items.description: The pair pleaded guilty after investigators linked them to the Scattered Spider cybercrime group, which US prosecutors say extorted dozens of companies.

items.title: Economic outlook is worsening and Trump is getting blamed, CNBC survey finds
items.description: The public is as depressed about the economy as it has been since the years just after the pandemic, according to the All-America Economic Survey.

items.title: Inside the Chinese fraud rings stealing billions from banks and retailers
items.description: Chinese organized crime groups are making as much as $1 billion annually in tap-to-pay fraud schemes targeting retailers and banks.

items.title: Amazon's Zoox issues software recall after robotaxi drove into heavy smoke
items.description: Last month, an unoccupied Zoox robotaxi drove into an active emergency fire scene that was clouded with smoke, the company said.

items.title: Apple, Nvidia vie for title of world's most valuable company
items.description: Nvidia shares have underperformed in 2026 as Wall Street shifts to companies powering the infrastructure AI buildout.

items.title: California's billionaire tax: What's at risk for the biggest state economy in America
items.description: California's billionaire tax would generate quick revenue, but Norway's example shows the longer-term economic impact of targeting the wealthy is unclear.

items.title: Chinese automakers are taking on the UK — and many Brits are embracing it
items.description: The surge comes as China's auto exports have boomed in recent years as the country's appetite for new models has cooled.

items.title: Import prices post surprise gain as costs of goods from China hit highest since 2008
items.description: Import prices were up 0.3% for the month, as a drop in energy was more than offset by increases elsewhere.

items.title: Netflix stock falls as earnings forecast disappoints, company says it will give fewer engagement updates
items.description: The streaming giant said it would cut back on the frequency of its "What We Watched" reports, which provide a picture of engagement.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DfHQJwqiOzc! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: I racked up $35,000 in credit-card debt. Should I file for bankruptcy?
items.description: “I’m also considering working with a credit-counseling agency or enrolling in a hardship program.”

items.title: EchoStar’s stock has fallen alongside SpaceX’s — but it may now be worth another look
items.description: EchoStar’s stock has for months been seen as a way to more cheaply get exposure to SpaceX.

items.title: My dad wants to buy me a big suburban home — but I don’t want to live there. Am I ungrateful if I turn him down?
items.description: “I would rather have something small, on a huge piece of land in a more rural area.”

items.title: Iran war hasn’t hurt the U.S. economy too badly — but the danger isn’t over yet
items.description: The two main pillars of the economy — consumer spending and business investment — appear to have shrugged off early angst arising from Iran tensions.

items.title: Taylor Farms to recall produce that may be the cause of the cyclospora outbreak: report
items.description: The California-based company is one of the largest producers of salad greens in the U.S.

items.title: ‘I am at a loss’: My brother is dying. We are not close. What responsibility do I have to help him?
items.description: “He is now no longer able to think clearly or communicate his wishes.”

items.title: Why Trump’s speech on U.S. elections could be bad for markets
items.description: The president focused in part on accusing China of “sinister election meddling” in 2020.

items.title: SpaceX postponed a crucial launch — and now its stock is sliding further below the IPO price
items.description: Shares in SpaceX declined about 4% in premarket trading on Friday after aborted mission.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: US chip stocks head for worst week in more than a year
items.description: High-flying semiconductor shares fall as ‘momentum trades’ backfire

items.title: Robert Jenrick’s wife knew in 2024 about foreign payments to his top donor, lawyer claimed
items.description: Police are investigating the legality of donations to the politician during his Conservative Party leadership bid

items.title: Ukraine’s rift over battlefield strategy breaks into the open
items.description: Sacking of popular defence minister exposes divisions over how to fight Russia

items.title: Warsh’ington times
items.description: The Fed chair offered little new information to Congress on his plans to shake up the central bank

items.title: Jonathan Reynolds set to oversee beefed-up business department
items.description: Incoming prime minister Andy Burnham discussing plans to give ministry more powers over science and energy policy

items.title: Reformation IPO bets fast fashion can keep up with Big Fashion
items.description: Maker of eco-friendly clothes can hold its own against Spanish giant Inditex on several metrics

items.title: EU to give industry more time to pollute and more funds to clean up
items.description: Climate commissioner defends extension of free allowances to heavily emitting sectors

items.title: South East Water warns it is struggling to secure new funding
items.description: Utility blames public and political scrutiny for hurting investor confidence

items.title: Pentagon’s Escort Policy for Journalists Is Temporarily Restored
items.description: An appeals court said the Defense Department could require escorts for reporters who visit the Pentagon while The New York Times sues to overturn the rule.

items.title: America’s Enterprising Spirit Is Booming After Decades-Long Slump
items.description: Hundreds of thousands of people are creating companies each year, ending a decades-long drought.

items.title: Roger Summit, Who Invented an Early Online Search Service, Dies at 95
items.description: A research scientist at Lockheed, he came up with the idea for a computer system that would search scientific and technical literature almost instantaneously.

items.title: States Rush to Fill Graduate Loan Gap Opened by G.O.P. Budget Bill
items.description: Minnesota and Connecticut are two of the states expanding their loan options. “We see it as a substitute program,” one official said.

items.title: Big Tech Firms Like Oracle Turn to Bonds to Finance A.I. Data Centers
items.description: Risk is rising as big tech companies like Oracle — the ultimate financial source of the Ellison media empire — need to turn to the bond market for staggering sums to finance data centers.

items.title: Stocks Sink on Anxiety About Tech and A.I. Spending
items.description: A sell-off in Asia led by chipmakers spilled into Europe and U.S. markets on Friday.

items.title: A New Blow to Tracking Gun Sales
items.description: The House has passed a bill that would bar the use of merchant codes specifically for firearm retailers, an effort to pre-empt state adoption of the tool.

items.title: Poolhouse, a Tech-Infused Pool Hall Start-up, Raises $55 Million
items.description: Poolhouse, which was created by the founders of Topgolf and uses technology to track players’ scores, aims to sell its platform to others.
